info.flavor_text: Prismari students dance in the tension between passion and perfection.

info.name: Resculpt
info.oracle_text: Exile target artifact or creature. Its controller creates a 4/4 blue and red Elemental creature token.
info.flavor_text: Critics were struck first by the elemental's surprising grace, and then by its fists.

Target player draws two cards.
Flashback—{1}{U}, Pay 3 life. (You may cast this card from your graveyard for its flashback cost. Then exile it.)
info.flavor_text: "No fact escapes me. Why do you think you can?"

info.name: Mirrorwing Dragon
info.oracle_text:
Flying
Whenever a player casts an instant or sorcery spell that targets only this creature, that player copies that spell for each other creature they control that the spell could target. Each copy targets a different one of those creatures.

info.oracle_text:
{T}: Add {C}.
{1}, {T}: Add one mana of any color. When you spend this mana to cast your commander, scry X, where X is the number of times it's been cast from the command zone this game.

name: Prismari Artistry (Secrets of Strixhaven Commander Precon Decklist)
info.name: Dance with Calamity
info.oracle_text: Shuffle your library. As many times as you choose, you may exile the top card of your library. If the total mana value of the cards exiled this way is 13 or less, you may cast any number of spells from among those cards without paying their mana costs.
info.flavor_text: It was just another day on Innistrad.

name: Prismari Artistry (Secrets of Strixhaven Commander Precon Decklist)
info.name: Aether Gale
info.oracle_text: Return six target nonland permanents to their owners' hands.
info.flavor_text: The militia formed up in the central plaza awaiting the onslaught, but the giants never quite made it.

info.oracle_text:
Reach, deathtouch
When this creature enters, search your library and/or graveyard for a card named Command Tower, reveal it, and put it into your hand. If you search your library this way, shuffle.
info.flavor_text: The tower guards eventually gave up, figuring it was better at the job anyway.
